_comment: Vendored transition registry for the product-launch workflow — the curated Tier-B subset (transform/opacity/filter on the two frame clip wrappers #el-<id>, no overlay DOM, no per-frame cooperation). Each type carries a GSAP template and an equivalent anime.js v4 template; the transitions.mjs injector picks one per target composition's detected runtime and stamps it onto the master timeline (window.__timelines["main"] for GSAP, hyperframesAnime.get("main").instance for anime). Recipes originate from the shared catalog skills/hyperframes-animation/transitions/ (css-*.md) — keep in step if those change. Token placeholders the injector substitutes: __OLD__ (#el-<from>), __NEW__ (#el-<to>), __T__/__T_MS__ (overlap-start, seconds for GSAP / milliseconds for anime), __DUR__/__DUR_MS__ (this boundary's duration, seconds/ms), __DX__/__DXIN__ (horizontal travel + incoming offset, px — same for both engines), __DY__/__DYIN__ (vertical, px). anime templates use explicit [from, to] arrays (never an implicit from) because the boundary's start position __T_MS__ can be > 0, decompose transforms into anime's translateX/translateY/scale/scaleX keys (never a CSS `transform` string), and use tl.set(...) for immediate (non-tweened) property writes instead of a zero-duration tl.add(...). GSAP eases map to anime eases per packages/core/src/animation/easeMap.ts (power2 -> Cubic, power3 -> Quart family). transformOrigin values use percentage pairs ("0% 50%" / "100% 50%"), never CSS keyword syntax ("left center") — verified in real Chrome (anime.js 4.5.0) that keyword syntax silently resolves to (0,0) for both endpoints instead of parsing the keywords.

transitions.name: blur-crossfade
transitions.note: Default when the two frames' #root backgrounds differ a lot — the blur masks the background-color clash a plain crossfade would expose.
gsap_template: tl.to(__OLD__, { filter: "blur(10px)", scale: 1.03, opacity: 0, duration: __DUR__, ease: "power2.inOut" }, __T__);
tl.fromTo(__NEW__, { filter: "blur(10px)", scale: 0.97, opacity: 0 }, { filter: "blur(0px)", scale: 1, opacity: 1, duration: __DUR__, ease: "power2.inOut" }, __T__);
anime_template: tl.add(__OLD__, { filter: ["blur(0px)", "blur(10px)"], scale: [1, 1.03], opacity: [1, 0], duration: __DUR_MS__, ease: "inOutCubic" }, __T_MS__);
tl.add(__NEW__, { filter: ["blur(10px)", "blur(0px)"], scale: [0.97, 1], opacity: [0, 1], duration: __DUR_MS__, ease: "inOutCubic" }, __T_MS__);

transitions.name: push-slide
transitions.note: Directional. The injector picks __DX__/__DY__ from the direction and emits the horizontal OR vertical pair (not both).
gsap_template_horizontal: tl.fromTo(__NEW__, { x: __DXIN__, opacity: 1 }, { x: 0, duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.inOut" }, __T__);
gsap_template_vertical: tl.fromTo(__NEW__, { y: __DYIN__, opacity: 1 }, { y: 0, duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.inOut" }, __T__);

transitions.name: zoom-through
gsap_template: tl.to(__OLD__, { scale: 2.5, opacity: 0, filter: "blur(8px)", duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.in" }, __T__);
tl.fromTo(__NEW__, { scale: 0.5, opacity: 0, filter: "blur(8px)" }, { scale: 1, opacity: 1, filter: "blur(0px)", duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.out" }, __T__);
anime_template: tl.add(__OLD__, { scale: [1, 2.5], opacity: [1, 0], filter: ["blur(0px)", "blur(8px)"], duration: __DUR_MS__, ease: "inQuart" }, __T_MS__);
tl.add(__NEW__, { scale: [0.5, 1], opacity: [0, 1], filter: ["blur(8px)", "blur(0px)"], duration: __DUR_MS__, ease: "outQuart" }, __T_MS__);

transitions.name: squeeze
transitions.note: Old compresses to a vertical line on the left edge; new expands from the right edge. Incoming starts off (scaleX 0) so its higher-track stacking is harmless.
gsap_template: tl.to(__OLD__, { scaleX: 0, transformOrigin: "left center", duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.inOut" }, __T__);
tl.fromTo(__NEW__, { scaleX: 0, transformOrigin: "right center", opacity: 1 }, { scaleX: 1, transformOrigin: "right center", duration: __DUR__, ease: "power3.inOut" }, __T__);
